channel.title: Blog on Fabricio Jiménez Morales
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Fabricio Jiménez Morales

item.title: My Password Workflow with Raycast and Pass
item.description: I don't like browser password managers. I use `pass` and Raycast, and it's smooth

item.title: Overwhelmed at VivaTech
item.description: I unexpectedly got the chance to attend a gigantic tech event in Paris. Despite the size of the crowd, I saw amazing innovation.

item.title: Listing my Latest Blog Posts on my GitHub Profile
item.description:
In the last few months, I’ve been working extensively on my transition to Platform Engineering, and that includes my online profile. The very fact that you’re reading this means, I may modestly say, that it’s working!

Today, a short write-up about a nice improvement to my GitHub Profile: automatically listing my latest blog posts. Since I use the Hugo Papermod theme, I had to make sure the site was producing an RSS feed. This is achieved by ensuring that hugo.yaml has RSS as an element of outputs.home. Additionally, I added the RSS icon to my header by adding the following element to params.socialIcons:

item.title: I Write When I Think I Can't Think
item.description: There was an earthquake at home and I can't do much about it. I can't concentrate on my stuff, but writing helps.

item.title: The Commonplace Repo or (Show How You) Build in Public
item.description: Code snippets and experiments from continuous learning in a repo is the IT professional's equivalent of a commonplace book.

item.title: Container Networking by Hand
item.description: "Containers are just Linux." Yes, let's zoom in.

item.title: Dev Contaniners and SSH
item.description: Development containers don't need private keys explicitly to communicate with GitHub, and I find that pretty neat.

item.title: Two Linux Security Tips
item.description: Two small habits to improve Linux security

item.title: My First and Best Linux Lesson
item.description: Creating a new user in Linux by hand can teach you more than anything else if you're a beginner
